model_justification: All 8 major competitors use subscription models. Duolingo's freemium success ($748M revenue, 10.3M paid subscribers, 41% YoY growth) validates freemium for consumer edtech. Free tier drives adoption in price-sensitive K-12 market; paid tier unlocks full AI tutoring across all subjects. Khanmigo (free for teachers, $4/mo parents), Chegg ($15-20/mo), Photomath ($9.99/mo) all use subscription. Freemium is the dominant approach with proven conversion benchmarks.

pricing_suggestion.justification: Positioned at mid-to-high of competitive range ($4-$180/mo) based on high pain_intensity from Problem validation. Parents currently spend $50-150/hr on human tutors, validating willingness to pay for effective alternatives. Price above Khanmigo ($4) and Chegg ($15-20) but well below human-tutoring hybrids ($180/mo). The 'replaces human tutors entirely' value prop justifies premium over basic AI tools.

unit_economics.churn_source: EdTech SaaS benchmark: 9.6% monthly average (wearefounders.uk 2026 benchmarks, mrrsaver.com). Adjusted to 7% assuming better-than-average retention from replacing high-cost human tutors (stickiness incentive) but acknowledging seasonal K-12 patterns.
unit_economics.margin_source: AI-first SaaS gross margins 55-70% (the-marketinghub.com, getmonetizely.com 2026). Traditional SaaS 75-85% minus ~5-10pp for AI compute costs (LLM inference per tutoring session). Used 70% as upper bound for AI-intensive product with significant per-user token consumption.
unit_economics.cac_source: B2C EdTech CAC benchmark $120-$150 (Deloitte via userpilot.com, financialmodelslab.com). Used $120 assuming PLG/freemium acquisition model with content marketing and organic virality (lower than paid acquisition). K-12 self-serve PLG range $50-$100 per grahamforman.medium.com.

cac_plus_20.assessment: CAC increase to $144 pushes LTV/CAC well below 2.0x threshold, indicating fragile unit economics with limited margin for acquisition cost increases.

churn_plus_20.assessment: Churn increase to 8.4% monthly compresses LTV to $166.58, pushing ratio below 2.0x. Given edtech's structural churn challenges (seasonal budgets, semester cycles), this scenario is plausible.

price_minus_20.assessment: Price reduction to $15.99/mo severely compresses margins. With 3+ free alternatives (ChatGPT, Khan Academy, Socratic), downward pricing pressure is a real risk.

model_precedents.segment: Consumer language learning (adjacent to K-12 education)
model_precedents.evidence: $748M revenue in 2024 (+41% YoY), 10.3M paid subscribers (+40% YoY), GAAP net income $88.6M. Subscription revenue $607.5M (+50% YoY). Freemium model with 5-7% conversion rate.
2.0M users in SY24-25, 731% YoY signup growth. $4/mo subscription, free for teachers. 700K K-12 students, expected 1M+ in 2025-26. Nonprofit with $107.3M total Khan Academy revenue.

model_precedents.segment: Online education platform (adjacent, includes K-12 content)
model_precedents.evidence: $694.7M in 2024 revenue, 113M registered users, 3000 enterprise customers. Coursera Plus subscription model increased LTV and reduced churn vs one-off purchases.

model_precedents.segment: K-12 and college homework help (direct match)
model_precedents.evidence: Public company (NYSE: CHGG), $15-20/mo subscription, 3.2M subscribers. However, in severe decline: revenue -30% YoY, stock -99% from peak due to AI disruption.
$65.3M funding, $44.9M revenue, 5M+ educators. Freemium model with district licensing. Could expand to student-facing AI tutoring.
YC S23 backed, 5M+ problems solved, 250K+ downloads. $6.99/week subscription. Covers math, chemistry, physics, biology, reading.

assumptions: Churn rate 7% monthly: adjusted downward from 9.6% edtech average assuming better retention from replacing expensive human tutors, but still high due to seasonal K-12 patterns (source: wearefounders.uk, mrrsaver.com edtech benchmarks)

Gross margin 70%: AI-first SaaS benchmark 55-70%, used upper bound assuming some model optimization but acknowledging heavy per-session LLM inference costs (source: the-marketinghub.com, getmonetizely.com)
CAC $120: B2C edtech benchmark, assuming PLG/freemium acquisition with content marketing as primary channel (source: Deloitte via userpilot.com, financialmodelslab.com)
Freemium conversion rate ~5%: industry standard for consumer SaaS with freemium model, based on Duolingo's demonstrated conversion rates
Price point $19.99/mo: mid-to-high of competitive range ($4-$180/mo) calibrated by high pain_intensity from Problem validation
ARPU based on paying users only ($19.99/mo): blended ARPU across all users would be ~$1/mo at 5% conversion, but unit economics calculated per paying customer
